title: AI's Biggest Day: Gemini 3.5 Pro Looms, WAIC Opens, Foldables Incoming
intro: Today lands on the busiest date of the AI calendar: a rumored Gemini 3.5 Pro drop, the world's first self-branded 'AI phone' at Shanghai's WAIC, plus a Chrome speed trick, a clear-eyed model comparison, and Samsung's confirmed July 22 Unpacked.

posts.hook: Gemini 3.5 Pro is reportedly landing today
posts.body: Gemini 3.5 Pro is widely tipped to launch today, July 17, as Shanghai's World AI Conference opens. The chatter: a 2-million-token context window and Deep Think reasoning on the Ultra tier. One honest caveat though — Google hasn't confirmed any of it. No model card, no pricing page, no date. Treat the specs as leaks, not gospel. If it ships today, it's a genuinely big deal; if it slips again, remember it already moved from June. Exciting, but hold the hype until there's an official page to point at.

posts.hook: Nubia is unveiling the 'world's first AI phone' today
posts.body: Nubia is officially unveiling what it's calling the world's first AI-powered smartphone today at WAIC 2026 in Shanghai. The pitch is a phone that completes tasks for you with deep AI baked into app control, not just another chatbot bolted on. The label is marketing — every flagship already ships AI features — but the idea of the OS itself acting as an agent is the direction the whole industry is walking toward. Worth watching what's real versus what's a demo.

posts.hook: Chrome's address bar has hidden '@' shortcuts
posts.body: Chrome's address bar can search inside itself. Type @tabs to jump to any open tab, @history to dig through pages you've visited, or @bookmarks to pull up a saved link — all without leaving the omnibox. Hit space after the @ keyword and start typing. If you're the type who keeps 40 tabs open, @tabs alone will save you a genuine amount of hunting every day. Free, already built in, and almost nobody uses it.

posts.hook: Gemini 3.5 vs GPT-5.6 vs Claude Fable 5 — which one to actually use
posts.body: Three AI titans, three different jobs. Claude Fable 5 holds the coding crown at 80.3% on SWE-Bench Pro and shines on long agentic runs — but it's the priciest at $10 / $50 per million tokens. GPT-5.6 (Sol, Terra, Luna) is the value all-rounder, ChatGPT's new default, with a 1M-token context and strong multi-agent tooling from around $1 per million input. Gemini 3.5 Pro, if today's leaks hold, brings a rumored 2M-token window and Deep Think — though those specs are still unconfirmed. The takeaway: there's no single best. Match the model to the task, not the headline.

posts.hook: Samsung Unpacked is officially set for July 22
posts.body: It's official: Samsung Galaxy Unpacked lands July 22 in London, with the Galaxy Z Fold 8, Z Flip 8, new Watches, and reportedly Samsung's first smart glasses. The interesting bit is the Fold 8's rumored shorter, wider shape — a real design rethink rather than a spec bump. Foldables have plateaued on 'thinner and pricier'; a genuinely new form factor plus a glasses play is Samsung trying to make the category exciting again. Pre-orders expected July 22, sales around August 7.
